steps.title: Use hemisphere volume
steps.content:
A hemisphere has half the volume of a sphere. With radius $r$, its volume is
$$\frac{1}{2}(\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3)=\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$

steps.title: Substitute the radius
steps.content:
Use $r=24$ in the hemisphere volume formula.
$$V=\frac{2}{3}\pi(24)^3$$
$$V=\frac{2}{3}\pi(13{,}824)$$
$$V=9{,}216\pi$$

steps.title: Estimate the volume
steps.content: Use $3.14$ as an approximation for $\pi$. $$9{,}216(3.14)=28{,}938.24$$ This is closest to $29{,}000$ cubic inches among the listed choices. The answer is D.
